title: T-101 — Add npd permission strings to enum (§2.2, §17.9, §18, §19)
prompt:
# T-101 — Add npd permission strings to enum

## Goal
Append a delimited `NPD` permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` covering the 17 canonical RBAC strings declared by PRD `docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md` (§2.2, §17.9, §18, §19). Settings/Auth owns the enum file; this task only contributes a new namespaced section under the `npd.*` prefix. Export `ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS` so the ESLint enum-lock guard (02-settings T-130) recognizes the module group.

## Implementation contract
- File: `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` — append entries to `Permission` const object with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor. Do NOT remove or rename any existing string.
- File: `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — extend the suite to assert presence, regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, uniqueness, and `ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS` typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 17.
- File: `CODEOWNERS` — verify `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` line exists; if missing, add it (already present today).

## Permission strings (17)
- `brief.create`
- `npd.project.delete`
- `npd.core.write`
- `npd.dashboard.view`
- `npd.d365_builder.execute`
- `npd.closed_flag.unset`
- `npd.schema.edit`
- `npd.rule.edit`
- `npd.risk.write`
- `npd.compliance_doc.write`
- `npd.formulation.create_draft`
- `npd.formulation.lock`
- `npd.recipe.submit_for_trial`
- `npd.pilot.promote_to_bom`
- `npd.gate.advance`
- `npd.gate.approve`
- `npd.bom.export`

## Files in scope
- `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` [modify]
- `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` [modify]
- `CODEOWNERS` [modify if missing entry]

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
- AC1 — Given the enum file is imported, when the consumer reads the new keys, then all 17 strings listed above are present exactly once.
- AC2 — Given each new permission string is matched against regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, when tested, then every string passes and zero duplicates exist across the union of `Object.values(Permission)`.
- AC3 — Given the module export, when imported, then `ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS` is exported as typed `readonly Permission[]` with length === 17.
- AC4 — Given `CODEOWNERS` is read, when checked, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` is architect-only (line `/packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts @monopilot/architect` present).

## Test strategy (RED first, vitest)
1. Extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with the four ACs above. Run RED:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`
   — must fail before any enum change.
2. Implement minimum changes in `permissions.enum.ts`.
3. Re-run vitest — must pass.
4. Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in the changed package; both must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not weaken the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` or introduce a 4-segment / matrix permission model.
- Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module `*PermissionEnum` in another file).
- Do not remove or rename existing strings (Settings/Foundation strings stay intact).
- Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
- Do not commit secrets or vault keys.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact RED→GREEN test commands and outputs
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

pipeline_inputs.description: Add 17 `npd.*` permission strings to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts and export ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS; closes RBAC enum-lock gap flagged in _meta/audits/2026-05-14-prd-vs-tasks-coverage-gaps.md for module 01-npd.
pipeline_inputs.details: Strings (count=17): ['brief.create', 'npd.project.delete', 'npd.core.write', 'npd.dashboard.view', 'npd.d365_builder.execute', 'npd.closed_flag.unset', 'npd.schema.edit', 'npd.rule.edit', 'npd.risk.write', 'npd.compliance_doc.write', 'npd.formulation.create_draft', 'npd.formulation.lock', 'npd.recipe.submit_for_trial', 'npd.pilot.promote_to_bom', 'npd.gate.advance', 'npd.gate.approve', 'npd.bom.export']. Rationale: 17 permission strings closing the gap flagged in audit §01-NPD: PRD §2.2 lists 19 capabilities; FG_CREATE/FG_EDIT/BRIEF_CONVERT_TO_NPD_PROJECT are already in the enum (with legacy aliases `fa.create` / `fa.edit` / `brief.convert_to_fa`). This task adds the remaining 17, all namespaced under `npd.*` or `brief.*` so workflow/SoD semantics keep the Settings-owned `npd.released_product_edit.*` strings (already in Settings T-002) intact. Same conventions as 02-settings T-001/T-002: const object with JSDoc per entry citing the PRD anchor, typed `Permission` union via `(typeof Permission)[keyof typeof Permission]`, and `ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS` declared `as readonly Permission[]`.
out_of_scope: Do not introduce a 4-level matrix permission model.
Do not edit other modules' permission strings.
Do not move permissions into Reference.RolePermissions table (enum is authoritative; the table is only the role→permission assignment mapping).
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Settings owns the enum file; this task appends a delimited namespaced group following the T-001 pattern.

ESLint enum-lock guard (`no-direct-permissions-enum-edit`) registered in packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs recognises module groups via ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS exports — this task adds the corresponding ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS export. (Previously mis-anchored to T-046, which is SET-006 Onboarding Completion; corrected to T-130 per fixer F4.)

acceptance_criteria: All 17 permission strings listed in pipeline_inputs.permission_strings are present in packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts, with JSDoc citing the PRD anchor.
Every new string matches regex ^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$ and no duplicates exist across Object.values(Permission); vitest assertion enforces both.
ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS is exported from packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts as `readonly Permission[]` with length === 17; type-check passes.
test_strategy: RED: extend packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts with presence + regex + uniqueness + array-length assertions for the 17 new strings. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`; expect failure.
GREEN: append the strings + ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS export; re-run vitest; expect pass.
Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` after the change; both must pass.
risk_red_lines: Do not introduce a parallel permission model (no per-module *PermissionEnum file).
Do not remove or rename existing strings.
Do not edit permission strings owned by other modules.
Do not couple enum strings to specific UI components or table rows.
